name: Genesis 6 9
shortDescription: Genesis 6-9 recounts the biblical flood narrative, depicting God's decision to cleanse the Earth of wickedness through a cataclysmic flood and Noah's subsequent survival with his family and animals.
description: Genesis 6-9 details the story of Noah's Ark and the Great Flood, a pivotal narrative in Jewish mythology and the broader Abrahamic traditions. It begins with God observing the corruption and violence that had overtaken humanity, leading Him to regret creating humankind. God resolves to wipe out all living creatures from the face of the Earth, with the exception of Noah, a righteous man, and his family. God instructs Noah to build an ark, specifying its dimensions and materials, and to bring aboard his wife, his sons, and their wives, along with pairs of all living creatures to preserve them from the impending deluge. The flood lasts for forty days and forty nights, covering the entire Earth and destroying all life outside the ark. After the waters recede, the ark comes to rest on Mount Ararat. Noah sends out birds to determine if the land has dried, and eventually, he and his family disembark, offering a sacrifice to God. God makes a covenant with Noah and all living creatures, promising never again to destroy the Earth by flood, and establishes the rainbow as a symbol of this covenant. The narrative explores themes of divine judgment, repentance, redemption, and the establishment of a new beginning for humanity. It is a foundational story that continues to resonate within Jewish theology and culture, addressing questions of morality, divine power, and the relationship between God and humankind.

sources.title: Noah's Flood: The Genesis Story in Western Thought
sources.author: Norman Cohn
